summary: Ukraine's escalating drone campaign against Russian logistics and energy infrastructure has reached a new intensity, with the Kapotnya refinery in Moscow struck a second time on June 18 — the largest drone attack on the capital in two years (RFE/RL, June 18) — while fuel rationing is now confirmed in over 50 Russian regions, including severe restrictions in Crimea and Donetsk. Sevastopol's Autoritet (Russian-installed) authority has tightened rationing to 20 liters per week with QR-code purchase controls, and ISW's June 18 Occupation Update confirms 80% of Crimea's June tourist bookings have been cancelled, threatening up to 18% of the peninsula's annual revenue. Russian Black Sea Fleet command structures are reportedly preparing to relocate remaining units from Sevastopol to Novorossiysk, with ATESH partisan networks recording accelerating family evacuations by occupation officials, many targeting completion before June 21 [unverified - Ukrainian partisan source]. The ISW June 18 update also highlights Russia's lowering of Free Economic Zone investment barriers in occupied Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhia, and Kherson — a sign that current investment attraction efforts are failing — while Sberbank continues its aggressive expansion, now reporting over 1 million customers in occupied Luhansk Oblast alone.
